_meta.pageRef: Specification at a Glance — Components 01 and 02
Specification at a Glance — Content Overview

topics.title: Systems architecture
topics.description: Component 01 topic. The purpose and function of the CPU, the fetch-execute cycle, CPU components and their functions, Von Neumann architecture, and factors affecting CPU performance.

subtopics.title: The purpose of the CPU
subtopics.description: The role of the CPU to fetch and execute instructions stored in memory. Von Neumann architecture.

subtopics.title: CPU components and their functions
subtopics.description: ALU, CU, cache, registers (MAR, MDR, program counter, accumulator) and their roles in the fetch-execute cycle.

subtopics.title: Factors affecting CPU performance
subtopics.description: Clock speed, number of cores, cache size and their effect on CPU performance.

topics.title: Memory and storage
topics.description: Component 01 topic. Primary storage (RAM and ROM), secondary storage types and uses, virtual memory, data storage and compression.

_meta.pageRef: Component 01: Computer systems — Memory and storage (1.2)

subtopics.title: Primary storage
subtopics.description: The need for primary storage; RAM (volatile, read/write) and ROM (non-volatile, read only); virtual memory.

subtopics.title: Secondary storage
subtopics.description: The need for secondary storage; types including optical, magnetic and solid state; suitability for different use cases.

subtopics.title: Units, data storage and compression
subtopics.description: Units of data storage (bits, bytes, kilobytes etc.); calculating file sizes; lossless and lossy compression.

topics.title: Computer networks, connections and protocols
topics.description: Component 01 topic. Types of network, factors affecting network performance, network hardware, topologies, protocols, and layers.

subtopics.title: Networks and topologies
subtopics.description: LAN and WAN; wired vs wireless; factors affecting network performance; star and mesh topologies.

subtopics.title: Network hardware
subtopics.description: Routers, switches, WAPs, NIC; the role of each device in a network.

subtopics.title: Protocols and layers
subtopics.description: TCP/IP, HTTP, HTTPS, FTP, POP, IMAP, SMTP; the concept of layers; the role of protocols.

topics.title: Network security
topics.description: Component 01 topic. Threats to computer systems and networks, and methods of prevention and protection.

subtopics.title: Threats to computer systems and networks
subtopics.description: Malware types (viruses, ransomware, spyware, adware, trojans, worms); social engineering attacks (phishing, pharming); brute force attacks; denial of service; SQL injection; man-in-the-middle attacks.

subtopics.title: Prevention methods
subtopics.description: Anti-malware, firewalls, encryption, strong passwords, two-factor authentication, biometrics, CAPTCHA, email filtering, automatic software updates.

topics.title: Systems software
topics.description: Component 01 topic. The role of the operating system and utility software.

subtopics.title: Operating systems
subtopics.description: The purpose and role of an operating system: user interface, memory management, multitasking, peripherals and drivers, user and file management.

subtopics.title: Utility software
subtopics.description: The purpose of utility software including: encryption software, defragmentation, data compression.

topics.title: Ethical, legal, cultural and environmental impacts
topics.description: Component 01 topic. The impact of digital technology on society, legislation and ethical issues.

subtopics.title: Ethical and cultural issues
subtopics.description: Ethical issues in computing; impacts of technology on society and culture; moral implications of AI, data collection, and automated systems.

_meta.pageRef: Component 01 — 1.6 Ethical, legal, cultural and environmental impacts

subtopics.title: Legal issues
subtopics.description: Key legislation including the Data Protection Act 2018, Computer Misuse Act 1990, Copyright Designs and Patents Act 1988, and the Freedom of Information Act 2000.

_meta.pageRef: Component 01 — 1.6 Ethical, legal, cultural and environmental impacts

subtopics.title: Environmental impacts
subtopics.description: The environmental impact of digital systems: energy usage, e-waste, manufacturing and disposal of hardware.

_meta.pageRef: Component 01 — 1.6 Ethical, legal, cultural and environmental impacts

topics.title: Algorithms
topics.description: Component 02 topic. Computational thinking, designing algorithms, searching and sorting algorithms, and trace tables.

subtopics.title: Computational thinking
subtopics.description: Decomposition, abstraction, and algorithmic thinking as key principles of computational thinking.

subtopics.title: Designing and refining algorithms
subtopics.description: Pseudocode and flowcharts to represent algorithms; identifying and correcting errors in algorithms.

subtopics.title: Searching and sorting algorithms
subtopics.description: Linear search and binary search; bubble sort, merge sort, and insertion sort — how they work and their relative efficiency.

topics.title: Programming techniques
topics.description: Component 02 topic. Core programming constructs, data structures, file handling, and programming paradigms.

subtopics.title: Programming constructs
subtopics.description: Sequence, selection (if/elif/else, switch/case), and iteration (for and while loops); variables, constants, and data types.

subtopics.title: Data structures
subtopics.description: Arrays (one and two-dimensional); records; files; use of appropriate data structures in programs.

subtopics.title: Procedures and functions
subtopics.description: Use of procedures and functions to modularise programs; parameters; return values; local and global variables.

subtopics.title: String handling
subtopics.description: String operations: length, substring, concatenation, upper/lower case conversion, ASCII conversion.

subtopics.title: File handling
subtopics.description: Reading from and writing to text files; opening and closing files.

subtopics.title: Robust programs
subtopics.description: Input validation; authentication; testing (types of testing, test plans, test data); identifying and fixing errors.

topics.title: Producing robust programs
topics.description: Component 02 topic. Defensive design considerations, testing and maintainability of programs.

_meta.pageRef: Component 02: Computational thinking — Producing robust programs (2.3)

subtopics.title: Defensive design
subtopics.description: Anticipating misuse; input sanitisation and validation; planning for contingencies.

subtopics.title: Testing
subtopics.description: Types of testing: iterative and final testing; test data types (normal, boundary, erroneous); trace tables; syntax and logic errors.

topics.title: Boolean logic
topics.description: Component 02 topic. Logic gates, truth tables, and Boolean expressions.

subtopics.title: Logic gates
subtopics.description: AND, OR, NOT, NAND, NOR, and XOR gates; their symbols, truth tables, and combinations.

subtopics.title: Boolean expressions
subtopics.description: Writing and evaluating Boolean expressions; applying Boolean algebra; constructing and reading logic diagrams.

topics.title: Languages and Integrated Development Environments
topics.description: Component 02 topic. Types of programming languages, translators, and the features of IDEs.

_meta.pageRef: Component 02: Computational thinking — Languages and IDEs (2.5)

subtopics.title: Types of programming languages
subtopics.description: High-level vs low-level languages; assembly language; machine code; advantages and disadvantages of each.

subtopics.title: Translators
subtopics.description: Compilers, interpreters, and assemblers — their purpose, differences, and when each is used.

subtopics.title: Integrated Development Environments (IDEs)
subtopics.description: The features of an IDE: editor, error diagnostics, run-time environment, translator, auto-completion, auto-indentation.

topics.title: Data representation
topics.description: Component 01 topic. Binary, hexadecimal, ASCII, Unicode, images, and sound representation.

subtopics.title: Number systems
subtopics.description: Binary, denary and hexadecimal number systems; conversion between bases; binary arithmetic (addition, overflow); two's complement for negative numbers.

subtopics.title: Character encoding
subtopics.description: ASCII and Unicode character sets; the need for Unicode over ASCII; representing characters in binary.

subtopics.title: Images and sound
subtopics.description: Representing images using pixels: resolution, colour depth, file size. Representing sound: sampling rate, bit depth, file size calculations.

_meta.pageRef: Specification at a Glance — Assessment Overview

sections.title: Section A — Short answer questions
sections.description: Short answer questions covering all Component 01 topics: systems architecture, memory and storage, data representation, networks, security, and systems software.

sections.title: Section A — Computational thinking and algorithms
sections.description: Questions on algorithms, computational thinking, logic, and translators.

sections.title: Section B — Programming
sections.description: Extended programming questions; students are expected to have practical programming skills and will write, trace, and correct programs.

_meta.pageRef: Specification at a Glance — Assessment Objectives

objectives.title: AO1 — Demonstrate knowledge and understanding
objectives.description: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the principles and concepts of computer science, including abstraction, logic, algorithms, and data representation.

_meta.pageRef: Specification at a Glance — Assessment Objectives

objectives.title: AO2 — Apply knowledge and understanding
objectives.description: Apply knowledge and understanding of computer science principles and concepts in a variety of contexts, including problem-solving, designing algorithms, and analysing programs.

_meta.pageRef: Specification at a Glance — Assessment Objectives

objectives.title: AO3 — Evaluate and improve
objectives.description: Evaluate problems, solutions, and the impact of computing; design, write, test and refine programs; select and justify the choice of algorithms and data structures.

_meta.pageRef: Specification at a Glance — Assessment Objectives
